steps.title: Spot the comma splice
steps.content: The original sentence has two independent clauses separated by a comma after “microscope”—one describing what Wigan is known for, the other describing what his sculptures are made from. A comma alone cannot properly join independent clauses, creating a comma splice.

steps.title: Use a period to establish sentence boundaries
steps.content:
A period fixes a comma splice by establishing a proper sentence boundary.

Option C uses a period after “microsculptures,” separating the two independent clauses into complete sentences.

Options A, B, and D fail to resolve this—A is ungrammatical, while B and D leave the comma splice intact.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
